Module com.example.cincuentazo
Package com.example.cincuentazo.model

Class Card

java.lang.Object
com.example.cincuentazo.model.Card
public final class Card extends Object
Represents a single immutable playing card, defined by its suit and rank.

A card knows how to report the value it contributes to the table sum according to the rules of Cincuentazo. Since the Ace can contribute either 1 or 10, the card exposes both its minimum and maximum value. The decision of which value to use is left to the game logic.

Cards are immutable: once created, their suit and rank never change. This makes them safe to store in any data structure and to share between the deck, the players' hands and the table.

  • Constructor Details

    • Card

      public Card(Suit suit, Rank rank)
      Creates a new card with the given suit and rank.
      Parameters:
      suit - the suit of the card; must not be null
      rank - the rank of the card; must not be null
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if suit or rank are null

  • Method Details

    • getSuit

      public Suit getSuit()
      Returns the suit of this card.
      Returns:
      the card suit

    • getRank

      public Rank getRank()
      Returns the rank of this card.
      Returns:
      the card rank

    • getMinValue

      public int getMinValue()
      Returns the minimum value this card contributes to the table sum.
      Returns:
      the minimum value

    • getMaxValue

      public int getMaxValue()
      Returns the maximum value this card contributes to the table sum.
      Returns:
      the maximum value

    • hasFlexibleValue

      public boolean hasFlexibleValue()
      Indicates whether this card has a flexible value (only Aces do).
      Returns:
      true if the card can take more than one value

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object other)
      Compares this card with another object for equality. Two cards are equal if they share the same suit and rank.
      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
      Parameters:
      other - the object to compare against
      Returns:
      true if both cards have the same suit and rank

    • hashCode

      public int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code consistent with equals(Object).
      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object
      Returns:
      the hash code of this card

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a human-readable representation of this card, useful for debugging and console output.

      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      a string describing the card
